content: Hey, I just finished reading 'The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks' and it blew my mind. Have you read it?
I haven't read it yet, but I've heard amazing things! What did you find most compelling about it?
The way it weaves together science, ethics, and personal history is just incredible. Made me think a lot about medical consent in my own work as a pediatrician.
That's fascinating. As a pediatrician, what are some of the biggest ethical considerations you face in your practice?
Oh man, so many. Vaccine hesitancy, parental refusal of treatment, explaining complex diagnoses to kids and families. It's a constant balancing act.
That sounds incredibly challenging. How do you typically approach those difficult conversations?
I try to meet families where they are, use age-appropriate language, and always involve the child when possible. Speaking of kids, I need to get Finn to the dog park before my shift.
Yeah, I adopted him from the shelter last month. Golden retriever, total sweetheart. He's my running buddy for marathon training too.
That's awesome! How's marathon training going? Any races coming up?
I'm training for the Twin Cities Marathon in October. It's brutal but I love it. Helps me decompress after long days at the clinic.
I can imagine! Do you listen to anything while you run? Podcasts, music?
Definitely podcasts. I'm obsessed with 'Radiolab' and 'This American Life'. Perfect for long runs through the trails near my place.
Those are great choices! Any other hobbies you're into?
I've gotten really into beekeeping lately. Started a hive in my backyard. It's surprisingly meditative and the honey is amazing.
Beekeeping sounds incredible! How did you get into that?
